_comment: Support Coordinator reference copy. Use conditional funding language only. Page shell: support-coordinators.html (when published).

meta.title: Information for Support Coordinators | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Reference language for support coordinators — creative studio sessions, individualized creative media sessions, and community participation supports.

hero.title: Information for Support Coordinators
hero.subtitle: Reference materials for coordinators reviewing individualized creative media sessions, public-facing classes, and community participation supports through our creative studio.

whatWeProvide.title: What We Provide
whatWeProvide.intro: Who's to Say Foundation offers interest-driven, community-centered creative supports — not licensed day habilitation or clinical services.
items: Individualized or small-group creative media coaching in video, design, photography, audio, and studio production
Project-based skill-building tied to participant interests and ISP goals where applicable
Community participation through outings, showcases, and storytelling projects
Documentation of session focus, skills practiced, and participant-led outcomes when requested

sampleDescriptions.title: Digital Content Creator Session
sampleDescriptions.body: Individualized creative media session supporting the person in accessible video editing, digital design, photography, or basic studio production. Supports are goal-driven and interest-led, building communication, planning, and practical digital skills.

sampleDescriptions.title: Community Storytelling Session
sampleDescriptions.body: Community participation session combining a supported outing or event with follow-up storytelling (video, writing, or art). Emphasis on self-expression, social connection, and advocacy — aligned with community inclusion goals.

sampleDescriptions.title: Art Studio & Showcase Preparation
sampleDescriptions.body: Creative studio session supporting design and creation of digital or physical items, plus presentation practice for low-pressure showcases. Focus on skill development, social interaction, and self-esteem rather than commercial sales.

sampleDescriptions.title: Entertainment & On-Camera Skills
sampleDescriptions.body: Individualized session focused on communication and performance skills: teleprompter practice, on-camera segments, voice work, and media literacy. Outcomes include improved self-expression and a small set of media clips reflecting the person's interests.

invoiceDescriptions.title: Sample invoice line — single session
invoiceDescriptions.body: Individualized creative media coaching session (approx. 1–2 hours): interest-led digital storytelling / studio production support, skill-building, and participant documentation as applicable.

invoiceDescriptions.title: Sample invoice line — multi-session block
invoiceDescriptions.body: Creative studio session block: project-based skill-building in digital media, design, or community storytelling; supports aligned with ISP goals for communication, community participation, and independence.

documentation.title: Documentation Available
items: Session date, duration, and format (individual or small group)
Skills and tools covered during the session

fundingNote.title: Public Access & Funding
fundingNote.body: Some participants may be eligible for funding when approved through the individual's plan or funding source. Approval decisions are made by the applicable funding source, plan, or reviewer. Who's to Say Foundation does not guarantee approval or act as a funding authority.

nextPhase.title: Next-Phase Vision
nextPhase.body: We are building toward an expanded maker space and community studio — a future phase supported by fundraising, not a current licensed facility. Language should reflect creative studio and community participation, not day-program or clinical framing.

sidebar.contactIntro: Email or call with session format questions, documentation needs, or scheduling.
quickReferenceItems: Public-facing classes — small group, max 3 participants, when scheduled
sidebar.fundingCardBody: Always use conditional phrasing: "may be eligible," "when approved through the individual's plan or funding source."

finalCta.title: Questions about a specific participant?
finalCta.body: Reach out with goals, interests, and any documentation requirements.
